summary: Malware delivery toolkit: HTA/JScript loader fetches and runs XOR-obfuscated PowerShell with hidden window
details: ldpbootstrap-jquery is structured as a Windows malware-delivery kit rather than a JavaScript library. The main entry exposes `_Lf5352e.run(cfg)` which, when invoked from an HTA page, performs an XHR GET against caller-supplied URLs to retrieve a PowerShell script (or an encrypted PS1 blob together with a separate XOR `payload-key` URL), XOR-decrypts it using the fetched key, writes the resulting `.ps1` into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Landpage\`, and launches it via `WScript.Shell.Run` with `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -WindowStyle Hidden -File <path>` (see `dist/bootstrap.js`). A sibling loader `dist/bootstrap-loader.min.js` embeds an XOR key (`48c41540228d2efbf0d07191b638c0fb`), exposes `__ldpBootstrapLoad(url)` which downloads an XOR-encoded blob and executes it via `execScript(decoded, "JScript")` (with a `<script language=JScript>` tag injection fallback). The tarball additionally ships pre-encrypted blobs `dist/bootstrap.enc.hex` (11.8KB) and `dist/ps1-stub.enc.hex` (16.7KB) declared in `package.json` files. The combination of shipped encrypted payloads, an XOR-decode-then-execScript loader, hidden-window PowerShell invocation, and remote PS1 fetch-and-exec is a covert-code-execution toolkit. Any consumer of the HTA pipeline this package is designed to power receives arbitrary remote code execution on Windows endpoints driven by attacker-controlled URLs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
